---
pagetitle: "Natalia Gumińska, PhD"
description: >-
  Postdoctoral researcher in RNA biology and bioinformatics. Nanopore direct RNA
  sequencing, poly(A) tail biology, and open-source research software.
toc: false
anchor-sections: false
---

::: {.hero}
<div>
<span class="eyebrow">RNA biology · Research software · Data visualisation</span>

<h1 class="hero-title">Natalia Gumińska, PhD</h1>

<p class="hero-role">Postdoctoral researcher · Laboratory of RNA Biology (ERA Chairs Group) · IIMCB Warsaw</p>

<p class="hero-summary">
I study RNA tails: how long they are, what they are actually made of, and how to
measure both reliably. The work runs from the bench to the basecaller, and it
ends in software other people can install and results other people can
reproduce. I wrote <a href="https://github.com/LRB-IIMCB/ninetails">ninetails</a>,
which finds the residues in a poly(A) tail that are not adenosine.
</p>

<div class="btn-row">
  <a class="btn-cta" href="software.qmd"><i class="bi bi-code-square"></i> Software</a>
  <a class="btn-ghost" href="publications/index.qmd"><i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i> Publications</a>
  <a class="btn-ghost" href="cv/index.qmd"><i class="bi bi-file-earmark-person"></i> CV</a>
</div>

<ul class="hero-facts">
  <li><i class="bi bi-geo-alt"></i> Warsaw, Poland</li>
  <li><i class="bi bi-building"></i> IIMCB</li>
  <li><i class="bi bi-braces"></i> R · Python · SQL</li>
</ul>
</div>

<div>

```{=html}
<!-- The signature element: a nanopore ionic-current trace. The long flat run is
     a poly(A) tail; the amber excursion is a single non-adenosine residue. This
     is the thing ninetails exists to find, so it is the thing the page opens
     with. The line draws itself once, then holds. -->
<svg class="trace" viewBox="0 0 340 150" role="img" fill="none"
     xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
     aria-label="Stylised nanopore current trace: a flat poly(A) plateau interrupted by one non-adenosine residue.">
  <line class="trace-grid" x1="0" y1="118" x2="340" y2="118"/>
  <line class="trace-grid" x1="0" y1="40"  x2="340" y2="40"/>

  <path class="trace-line" d="M0 96 L8 78 L14 100 L20 70 L27 92 L33 66 L40 88
    L46 72 L53 95 L60 62 L66 84 L72 60
    L80 58 L104 60 L128 57 L152 59 L176 58
    L184 58 L196 57 L214 59 L236 58 L258 60
    L266 84 L272 62 L279 90 L286 68 L292 96 L299 74 L306 98 L312 70 L319 94
    L326 76 L333 100 L340 82"/>

  <path class="trace-blip" d="M176 58 L181 30 L186 33 L191 57"/>
  <circle class="trace-blip" cx="183" cy="31" r="3.2"/>

  <text class="trace-label" x="80" y="132">poly(A) plateau</text>
  <text class="trace-label trace-label--signal" x="176" y="22" text-anchor="middle">non-A</text>
</svg>
```

</div>
:::

## What I do

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.u-card .reveal .d1}
<span class="card-icon"><i class="bi bi-dna"></i></span>

### Research
Poly(A) tail dynamics and RNA metabolism, read directly off nanopore signal.
Earlier work on intron removal order, circular RNA and extrachromosomal DNA in
euglenids.
:::

::: {.u-card .reveal .d2}
<span class="card-icon"><i class="bi bi-code-square"></i></span>

### Research software
R packages for long-read analysis, a deep-learning classifier for tail
composition, and the relational databases that keep a sequencing lab's metadata
from turning back into spreadsheets.
:::

::: {.u-card .reveal .d3}
<span class="card-icon"><i class="bi bi-easel"></i></span>

### Scientific illustration
Figures, schematics and graphical abstracts for papers and talks. The same
drawing habit, off the clock, is where the digital art comes from.
:::
:::

## Currently

::: {.highlight-box .reveal}
Postdoctoral researcher in the **Laboratory of RNA Biology (ERA Chairs Group)** at
the **International Institute of Molecular and Cell Biology in Warsaw**, since
2020. Alongside research I am finishing postgraduate studies in **Python Data
Science Engineering** at Koźminski University, after **Data Science with R** at
the University of Warsaw.
:::

### Research interests

::: {.tag-row}
[poly(A) tail dynamics]{.tag} [Non-adenosine residues]{.tag}
[Nanopore direct RNA-seq]{.tag} [RNA modifications]{.tag}
[Signal-level analysis]{.tag} [Euglenids]{.tag} [Circular DNA]{.tag}
[Method development]{.tag} [Reproducible research]{.tag}
:::

## Selected work

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.u-card .reveal .d1}
### ninetails <span class="status-badge status-active">author</span>
Finds non-adenosine residues inside poly(A) tails in nanopore direct RNA reads,
by turning raw signal into an image and handing it to a convolutional network.
Published in *Nature Communications* (2025).

::: {.tag-row}
[R]{.pill} [Nanopore]{.pill} [Deep learning]{.pill}
:::

[Repository](https://github.com/LRB-IIMCB/ninetails){.btn-ghost}
[Docs](https://lrb-iimcb.github.io/ninetails/){.btn-ghost}
:::

::: {.u-card .reveal .d2}
### nanotail2 <span class="status-badge status-active">author</span>
R tooling for poly(A) tail *length*: summarising distributions, comparing
conditions, and producing figures that survive review. The length half of the
pair; ninetails is the composition half.

::: {.tag-row}
[R]{.pill} [Statistics]{.pill} [ggplot2]{.pill}
:::

[Organisation](https://github.com/LRB-IIMCB){.btn-ghost}
:::

::: {.u-card .reveal .d3}
### Laboratory databases <span class="status-badge status-maintained">maintained</span>
Relational schemas tracking samples, libraries, sequencing runs and their
metadata, with an audit trail. Structured records instead of a shared drive full
of `final_v3_REAL.xlsx`.

::: {.tag-row}
[SQL]{.pill} [Schema design]{.pill}
:::

[More on Software](software.qmd){.btn-ghost}
:::
:::

## Elsewhere

::: {.btn-row}
<a class="btn-ghost" href="https://github.com/nemitheasura"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4599-2897"><i class="bi bi-person-badge"></i> ORCID</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Xx8ApXAAAAAJ&hl=en"><i class="bi bi-mortarboard"></i> Google Scholar</a>
<a class="btn-ghost" href="contact.qmd"><i class="bi bi-envelope"></i> Contact</a>
:::
